existing_claim: Checked the scope claim that RG capture is excluded from this ADR's ground-truth-mutation invariant against ADR-001 §2.2 and ADR-002 §2.6. It holds: ADR-001 §2.2 frames RG content as captured reasoning governed by the reasoning-capture invariant and its per-reasoner authoritative flag, and ADR-002 §2.6 assigns RG write authority (ASA authors ReasoningProgress; AOE owns ReasoningSession lifecycle) — a distinct authority regime from ground-truth mutation. DDR-002 §4's accountability-boundary note ('RG content is captured judgment, not held to the KG determinism invariant') corroborates the line. The exclusion is consistent with the cited substrate.
incoming_claim: Attacked the scope claim that Reasoning-Graph capture is excluded from this ADR's ground-truth-mutation invariant, testing whether the exclusion drops a class of ground-truth-affecting writes. It held: ADR-001 §2.2 frames RG content as captured reasoning under the reasoning-capture invariant with its own per-reasoner authoritative flag, ADR-002 §2.6 assigns RG write authority (ASA authors ReasoningProgress; AOE owns ReasoningSession lifecycle) as a distinct regime, and DDR-002 §4's accountability-boundary note ('RG content is captured judgment, not held to the KG determinism invariant; the EA gate ... restores accountability and authoritativeness') confirms RG capture is not enterprise ground truth and its promotion into ground truth is exactly the Promotion class §2.2 governs. The boundary is drawn consistent with the cited substrate and leaves no ground-truth mutation path ungoverned.

existing_claim: I attacked §2.5 for internal contradiction with §2.2 — a §2.5 per-write 'blocked and surfaced' claim standing against §2.2's 'no per-write gate' for the Environment class. It holds: §2.5 explicitly distinguishes the routine (ungated) observation stream from the gateway-mechanical conflict-detection that fires only on structural collision with an EA-set governance scope, escalating that write to human resolution, and locates human accountability at the resolution step rather than at each observation. The two loci are reconcilable exactly as written — the collision case and the routine stream are named and separated. Checked for internal correctness against the SDD-001 §3.5.2 SCOPE_CONFLICT mechanical-block instance the ADR authorizes.
incoming_claim: Attacked §2.5 for internal contradiction with §2.2 — a §2.5 per-write 'blocked and surfaced' claim standing against §2.2's 'no per-write gate' for Environment. It held: §2.5 explicitly distinguishes the routine ungated observation stream from a gateway-mechanical conflict-detection (ADR-002 §2.6 sole executor) that fires only on structural collision with an EA-set governance scope, escalating that write to human resolution, and locates human accountability at the resolution step. The two loci are reconcilable exactly as written, and the shape matches the cited built interim instance (SDD-001 §3.5.2 SCOPE_CONFLICT mechanical block). No contradiction.

existing_claim: §1 states that downstream work is blocked on this record and that accepted records depend on the authority it homes, framing the ADR as the load-bearing dependency the corpus is waiting on. The record's own metadata carries Status: PROPOSED (v0.1.0). A PROPOSED record has not yet decided anything the corpus can bind to — the authority it purports to home is not established until acceptance. The Context claims a discharged-authority standing the record's own status does not yet support; the decision-content of what this record supplies to the corpus is stated in a tense its status has not earned.
incoming_claim: §1 states downstream work is blocked on this record and that accepted records depend on the authority it homes, framing the ADR as the load-bearing dependency the corpus awaits. existing_claim: §2.1 requires that a named human be answerable for every control, per-write or per-policy, and §6 check 1 mechanizes 'names the human accountable for it (per-policy).' For the Promotion, ingestion, and distillation classes the record names EA or a distinct reviewer; for the Environment per-policy control and the §2.5 cross-class resolution step it states only that a human is answerable, without fixing which role holds that accountability (EA? a config/governance owner?). The decision the record claims to make — that a named human is accountable for every class — is under-specified for the per-policy branch it introduces, so §6 check 1's own conformance criterion for that branch is under-determined against §2.1's 'named human' requirement.
incoming_claim: §2.1 requires that a named human be answerable for every control (per-write or per-policy), and §6 check 1 mechanizes 'names the human accountable for it (per-write or per-policy).' For the Promotion, ingestion, and distillation classes the record names EA or a distinct reviewer; for the Environment per-policy control it states only that 'A human is answerable for the weighting policy' and for the §2.5 cross-class resolution step only that 'a human resolves', without fixing which role holds that accountability. The decision the record claims to make — that a named human is accountable for every class — is under-specified for the per-policy branch it introduces, so §6 check 1's own conformance criterion for that branch is under-determined against §2.1's 'named human' requirement.

existing_claim: I attacked §2.5 for internal contradiction with §2.2 — a §2.5 per-write 'blocked and surfaced' claim standing against §2.2's 'no per-write gate' for the Environment class. It holds: §2.5 explicitly distinguishes the routine (ungated) observation stream from the gateway-mechanical conflict-detection that fires only on structural collision with an EA-set governance scope, escalating that write to human resolution, and locates human accountability at the resolution step rather than at each observation. The two loci are reconcilable exactly as written — the collision case and the routine stream are named and separated. Checked for internal correctness against the SDD-001 §3.5.2 SCOPE_CONFLICT mechanical-block instance the ADR authorizes.
incoming_claim: I attacked §2.5 for internal contradiction with §2.2 — a §2.5 'blocked and surfaced' per-write claim standing against §2.2's 'no per-write gate' for the Environment class, which would be a specified behavior that could not hold if implemented as written. It held: §2.5 explicitly distinguishes the routine ungated observation stream from a gateway-mechanical conflict-detection (ADR-002 §2.6 sole executor) firing only on structural collision with an EA-set governance scope, escalating that write to human resolution, with human accountability located at the resolution step. The two loci are jointly satisfiable exactly as written, and the shape matches the cited built interim instance (SDD-001 §3.5.2 SCOPE_CONFLICT mechanical block). No internal contradiction; cited instance resolves.

existing_claim: The ingestion authority record is referenced under two non-identical subject-names: 'the forthcoming ingestion decision record' (§2.2 table, §5.2) and 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record' (§7). Because both are subject-name forms (not concrete identifiers), a reader reconciling cross-references cannot verify whether they denote one artifact or two (a general ingestion record vs. a file-driven-specific one). The forward reference's subject-name is not stable across the loci that cite it — a downstream record cannot bind its concrete identifier at landing to a referent that is named ambiguously, so the pointer-resolution obligation is under-specified.
incoming_claim: The ingestion authority record this control's realization routes to is named non-identically across the loci that cite it: §2.2 table and §5.2 refer to 'the forthcoming ingestion decision record'; §2.2 prose and §7 refer to 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record.' A reader integrating this record's forward commitments cannot verify whether these denote one artifact or two (a general ingestion record vs a file-driven-specific one). An unstable subject-name for a forthcoming record leaves the pointer this ADR commits — where the authoritative-source ingestion control is realized — under-determined at the seam where the downstream record must bind back.

existing_claim: Attacked the integration posture: whether ADR-008 pulls against the prior ratified ADR-001 §2.5 by silently overriding it while claiming no supersession — a hard-to-reverse dual-authority conflict if the two disagree on the promotion gate. It held: §2.2 and §2.3 frame §2.5 as the narrow sub-case ADR-008 generalizes and homes ('cited, not superseded'), §2.1 scopes this record to ground-truth mutation while excluding RG capture (§2.5's separate authority regime), and §2.3 homes un-promotion as an extension §2.5 does not reach. 'Supersedes: None' is the correct posture for a new principle that subsumes a sub-case rather than replacing a record, and the dependency direction (downstream records cite this ADR) is coherent. No dual-authority conflict with the prior ratified decision.
incoming_claim: I attacked the integration posture: whether ADR-008 pulls against the prior ratified ADR-001 §2.5 by silently overriding it while claiming no supersession — a hard-to-reverse dual-authority conflict if the two disagree on the promotion gate. It held: §2.2 and §2.3 frame §2.5 as the narrow sub-case ADR-008 generalizes and homes ('cited, not superseded'), §2.1 scopes this record to ground-truth mutation while excluding RG capture (§2.5's separate authority regime), and §2.3 homes un-promotion as an extension §2.5 does not reach. 'Supersedes: None' is the correct posture for a new principle that subsumes a sub-case rather than replacing a record, and the dependency direction (downstream records cite this ADR) is coherent — no dual-authority conflict with the prior ratified decision.

existing_claim: §2.1 requires that a named human be answerable for every control (per-write or per-policy), and §6 check 1 mechanizes naming that human. For the Environment class the accountable act is a DDR-004 §6 calibration ratification, and for the cross-class withhold the accountable act is at the resolution step; the ADR states a human is answerable but nowhere fixes which role holds the per-policy Environment accountability, whereas the other three classes name EA or a distinct reviewer. §6 check 1's per-policy branch could pass a design asserting a nameless 'per-policy' accountability for Environment, leaving the check's per-policy criterion under-determined against §2.1's 'named human' requirement.
incoming_claim: §2.1 requires that a named human be answerable for every control (per-write or per-policy), and §6 check 1 mechanizes 'names the human accountable for it (per-policy).' For the Promotion, ingestion, and distillation classes the record names EA or a distinct human reviewer; for the Environment per-policy control and the §2.5 cross-class resolution step it states only that 'a human is answerable for the weighting policy' / that 'a human resolves', without fixing which role holds that per-policy accountability. §6 check 1's per-policy branch could therefore pass a design asserting a nameless per-policy accountability for Environment, leaving the check's conformance criterion under-determined against §2.1's 'named human' requirement.

existing_claim: Checked the scope claim that RG capture is excluded from this ADR's ground-truth-mutation invariant against ADR-001 §2.2 and ADR-002 §2.6. It holds: ADR-001 §2.2 frames RG content as captured reasoning governed by the reasoning-capture invariant and its per-reasoner authoritative flag, and ADR-002 §2.6 assigns RG write authority (ASA authors ReasoningProgress; AOE owns ReasoningSession lifecycle) — a distinct authority regime from ground-truth mutation. DDR-002 §4's accountability-boundary note ('RG content is captured judgment, not held to the KG determinism invariant') corroborates the line. The exclusion is consistent with the cited substrate.
incoming_claim: Attacked the §2.1 exclusion of Reasoning-Graph capture from this ADR's ground-truth-mutation invariant, testing whether a class of ground-truth-affecting writes is dropped by the exclusion. It held: ADR-001 §2.2 governs RG content as captured reasoning under the reasoning-capture invariant with its own per-reasoner authoritative flag; ADR-002 §2.6 assigns RG write authority (ASA authors ReasoningProgress, AOE owns ReasoningSession lifecycle) as a distinct regime; DDR-002 §4's accountability-boundary note confirms RG content is captured judgment, not KG ground truth, and its entry into ground truth is exactly the Promotion class §2.2 governs. No ground-truth mutation path is left ungoverned by the exclusion.
